game_name: Ultima IV: Quest of the Avatar

danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile creature sprites in overworld and dungeon spaces
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark cave or underground portal structures on map
danger_signals.meaning: Increased danger zone; presence of monsters and environmental hazards

danger_signals.visual_form: NPC dialogue text; Hawkwind's chastisement messages
danger_signals.meaning: Player character has strayed from moral path, losing spiritual progress

safety_signals.visual_form: Clustered settlement structures on overworld map
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones with NPCs, merchants, lodging; peace and civilization

safety_signals.visual_form: Central castle structure; seat of authority on map
safety_signals.meaning: Primary safe haven; source of quest guidance and spiritual counsel
Safe spiritual spaces for virtue development and enlightenment

safety_signals.visual_form: Hawkwind's feedback messages confirming enlightenment gains
safety_signals.meaning: Player is on correct moral path; spiritual growth is occurring

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Lord British's Castle as central authority symbol
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Unified kingdom cities; Renaissance Italy / Camelot-inspired architecture; established settlements across unified world

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: The Eight Virtues; Runes of each Virtue
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Eight shrines scattered throughout Britannia, each dedicated to one Virtue; Stygian Abyss as ultimate spiritual trial

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Thriving Renaissance-inspired cities; expanded world map (16x larger than Ultima III); established settlements replacing destroyed ones; unified Britannia under stable rule
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs are available for extended dialogue; focus shifts from combat survival to moral introspection; quest structure centered on spiritual growth rather than defeating tangible evil

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Spiritual vacuum after triumph over Triad of Evil
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lord British proclaims Quest of the Avatar; emphasis on shrine networks and virtue infrastructure; Codex of Ultimate Wisdom hidden in Stygian Abyss
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Population lacks purpose; player character must become Avatar example; NPCs seek spiritual guidance; world needs moral leadership

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: NPC interactions become more favorable; access to restricted shrines; progression toward Avatarhood
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs respond with respect; Hawkwind provides encouragement; eligible for shrine elevation when ready

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dialogue feedback shifts tone; loss of shrine access eligibility
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hawkwind chastises character for straying from path; NPC interactions become colder; must undertake corrective actions (alms-giving, combat valor, humility)

environmental_storytelling.example: Visual world map shows result of Triad defeat: three-quarters world disappeared, continents rose/sank, new cities built—geography itself tells story of radical post-war change
Britannia resembles Renaissance Italy or Camelot rather than dark age wasteland—visual aesthetic communicates prosperity and stability
Eight shrines scattered across world map communicate spiritual infrastructure; their locations teach players about geography while reinforcing virtue theme
Stygian Abyss structure and difficulty visually represent the final spiritual trial required for Avatarhood
NPCs discuss their lack of purpose post-war; dialogue reveals psychological and social world-state beyond visual environment

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Provides encouragement; informs when player is ready for shrine elevation; offers specific actions to improve standing
Chastises character; indicates how far player has fallen from Avatar path
Favorable interaction; responsive to dialogue options; treat player with respect
Colder interactions; may refuse quests or guidance; dialogue reflects moral judgment
Discuss their lack of purpose; seek guidance; indicate spiritual hunger of population
Attack on sight; require combat or evasion; scale up in difficulty in deeper dungeons

notes: Ultima IV pioneered moral manifestation systems in RPGs. World-state communication is primarily virtue-based rather than good-vs-evil faction-based. The game uses spiritual progression (Eight Virtues) as both narrative framework and mechanical system. Visual communication of danger/safety heavily dependent on location type (city vs. dungeon vs. wilderness) rather than faction markers. NPC behavioral response serves as key feedback mechanism for player moral state. The Renaissance/Camelot aesthetic communicates peaceful prosperity, shifting focus from combat-centric dungeon crawl to ethical philosophy and character development.
